[FIGURE] A schematic diagram with three vertical columns of colored rectangular nodes.  
- Top row: three gray boxes labeled o1, o2, o3.  
- Second row: three light-blue boxes labeled v1, v2, v3, aligned beneath the o boxes.  
- Third row: three light-orange boxes labeled k1, k2, k3, aligned beneath the v boxes.  
- Bottom row: three salmon-colored boxes labeled q1, q2, q3, aligned beneath the k boxes.  

Black connecting lines show relationships between nodes:
- o1 is connected vertically to v1.
- o2 is connected vertically to v2.
- o3 is connected vertically to v3.
- v1 connects diagonally up-right to o2.
- v1 connects diagonally up-right to o3.
- v2 connects diagonally up-right to o3.
- k1 is connected vertically to q1.
- k2 is connected vertically to q2.
- k3 is connected vertically to q3.
- k1 connects diagonally down-right to q2.
- k1 connects diagonally down-right to q3.
- k2 connects diagonally down-right to q3.

The diagram visually groups the o, v, k, and q nodes into four layers with vertical alignment and cross-layer diagonal links.

- v3 to o4
- v4 straight up to o4
- k1 to q4

A directed-like node diagram on a white background. Across the top/middle are five light-blue vertical boxes labeled v1, v2, v3, v4, v5. Directly below them are five yellow vertical boxes labeled k1, k2, k3, k4, k5. Near the upper-right is a gray vertical box labeled o5. Near the lower-right is a peach vertical box labeled q5.

Black connecting lines show relationships among the nodes: several lines run from v1, v2, v3, and v4 toward o5, and a vertical line connects o5 down to v5. From k1, k2, k3, and k4, several diagonal lines run down toward q5, and a vertical line connects k5 down to q5. The lines converge at o5 and q5, indicating many-to-one connections from the left-side nodes to those right-side nodes.

[FIGURE] A schematic diagram of repeated stacked rectangular blocks arranged in rows. The left side labels eight alternating rows: “value 1”, “key 1”, “value 2”, “key 2”, “value 3”, “key 3”, “value 4”, “key 4”. Each row contains multiple vertical rectangles across the slide, with light blue rectangles for value rows and light orange/yellow rectangles for key rows. In the middle of the grid there are four separate “……” ellipses indicating omitted intermediate columns/entries. On the far right, each key row is connected by a thick black horizontal line to a small peach-colored rectangle labeled respectively “query 1”, “query 2”, “query 3”, and “query 4”. The diagram visually shows each key row aligned to a corresponding query block on the right, with value rows interleaved between key rows.

SRAM
HBM

[FIGURE] On the left is a large explosion graphic labeled “HBM” underneath. On the right is a cartoon elf/woodworker labeled “SRAM” underneath, shown working at a tree stump with tools and small scattered items on the ground. Between them are two horizontal black arrows pointing in opposite directions: the upper arrow points from left to right, and the lower arrow points from right to left. Above the upper arrow is a small cartoon elf carrying a wooden crate; below the lower arrow is another small cartoon elf carrying a wooden crate. The overall layout visually compares HBM and SRAM with bidirectional arrows indicating exchange or movement between the two sides.

Gemma 2

Parameters	2B	9B	27B
d_model	2304	3584	4608
Layers	26	42	46
Pre-norm	yes	yes	yes
Post-norm	yes	yes	yes

Non-linearity	GeGLU	GeGLU	GeGLU
Feedforward dim	18432	28672	73728

Head type	GQA	GQA	GQA
Num heads	8	16	32
Num KV heads	4	8	16
Head size	256	256	128
Global att. span	8192	8192	8192
Sliding window	4096	4096	4096

Vocab size	256128	256128	256128
Tied embedding	yes	yes	yes

Each toke:
46 (layers) x 32 (heads) x 128
(dimension) x 2 (FP16) x 2 (value, key)
= 753664 bytes (736 KB, 0.72MB)

A100 (80GB):
About 114k tokens

[FIGURE]
A table titled “Gemma 2” compares three model sizes across columns labeled 2B, 9B, and 27B. It lists architecture and configuration rows: d_model, Layers, Pre-norm, Post-norm, Non-linearity, Feedforward dim, Head type, Num heads, Num KV heads, Head size, Global att. span, Sliding window, Vocab size, and Tied embedding. The values shown are:
- d_model: 2304, 3584, 4608
- Layers: 26, 42, 46
- Pre-norm: yes for all three
- Post-norm: yes for all three
- Non-linearity: GeGLU for all three
- Feedforward dim: 18432, 28672, 73728
- Head type: GQA for all three
- Num heads: 8, 16, 32
- Num KV heads: 4, 8, 16
- Head size: 256, 256, 128
- Global att. span: 8192 for all three
- Sliding window: 4096 for all three
- Vocab size: 256128 for all three
- Tied embedding: yes for all three

[FIGURE]
On the right side, a text calculation states: “Each toke: 46 (layers) x 32 (heads) x 128 (dimension) x 2 (FP16) x 2 (value, key) = 753664 bytes (736 KB, 0.72MB)”. Below it, another text block reads: “A100 (80GB): About 114k tokens”. This communicates an estimated per-token memory cost and the approximate token capacity of an A100 80GB GPU.

[FIGURE] Left-side diagram labeled “Multi-query attention”: one blue box labeled “value” and one yellow box labeled “key” on the left connect via black lines from a central point to four orange query boxes labeled “query 1”, “query 2”, “query 3”, and “query 4” arranged vertically on the right. The lines fan out from the single key/value pair to all four queries.

[FIGURE] Right-side diagram labeled “Group-query attention” with the text “Llama, Gemma, etc.” above it: two separate key/value pairs are shown. The upper pair is “value 1” (blue) above “key 1” (yellow), with black lines from the key to “query 1” and “query 2” (orange boxes) on the right. The lower pair is “value 2” (blue) above “key 2” (yellow), with black lines from the key to “query 3” and “query 4” (orange boxes) on the right.

[FIGURE]
A diagram illustrating “Multi-head Latent Attention.” On the left, a vertical stack of alternating light blue and orange rectangular blocks is grouped by a large black curly brace, with a small pink rectangle to the right of the brace near the middle, suggesting a compressed latent representation from multiple blocks. In the center-right, a tall green vertical rectangle acts as a source node. From this green rectangle, multiple black arrows fan out to the right toward a second vertical stack of alternating light blue and orange rectangular blocks. The right-side stack contains more blocks than the left-side grouped stack, and the arrows connect the central green source to each of the right-side blocks, indicating one-to-many expansion or distribution across multiple outputs/heads.

https://arxiv.org/
abs/2405.04434

[FIGURE] Diagram illustrating “Multi-head Latent Attention.” On the left, a vertical stack of alternating light-blue and orange rectangular blocks is grouped by a large curly brace; the brace points to a single small pink rectangle to its right, indicating multiple elements being compressed into one latent representation. In the center, a tall green vertical rectangle points via a rightward arrow to a small pink rectangle. From that pink rectangle, multiple black arrows fan out to the right toward another vertical stack of alternating light-blue and orange rectangles, showing one latent unit expanding or distributing information to many outputs.

[FIGURE] Left-side diagram: a pink box labeled “c” points by a thin arrow to a vertical stack of alternating blue and yellow rectangular blocks, with the central yellow block labeled “k”. Above the arrow is the equation “k = Wₖc”. To the right of the k stack is a thick horizontal line labeled “α” leading to an orange box labeled “q”. This illustrates c being transformed into k, and α shown between k and q.

[FIGURE] Right-side diagram: a pink box labeled “c” points by a thick diagonal line labeled “α” to a light purple box labeled “q′”. To the right is an orange box labeled “q”, with a thin left-pointing arrow from q toward q′. Under the arrow is the equation “q′ = Wₖᵀ q”. This shows q being transformed into q′ and the α relationship between c and q′.

α = q · k = qᵀk = qᵀWₖc

= (Wₖᵀq)ᵀc = (Wₖᵀq) · c

[FIGURE] A schematic diagram showing four parallel content vectors c1, c2, c3, c4 (small pink rectangles) on the left, each connected by a diagonal arrow to a corresponding transformed vector v1, v2, v3, v4 (blue rectangles) in the middle/right. The arrows are labeled α1, α2, α3, α4 respectively. Under each pair is the equation v1 = W_v c1, v2 = W_v c2, v3 = W_v c3, v4 = W_v c4. The upper part of the figure shows stacks of colored rectangles above each v_i, indicating vector blocks/components. The overall pointwise combination is written below as o = α1v1 + α2v2 + α3v3 + α4v4, then expanded to o = α1W_v c1 + α2W_v c2 + α3W_v c3 + α4W_v c4, and factored as o = W_v(α1c1 + α2c2 + α3c3 + α4c4).

Sliding Window Attention

[FIGURE]
A diagram showing three horizontal rows of small vertical yellow rectangles representing token positions. On the far right of each row is a salmon-colored vertical rectangle labeled “query”. Black rounded rectangular outlines highlight small local windows over pairs of adjacent yellow rectangles in each row. Black arrows connect the highlighted window in one row to the next, illustrating how the attention window shifts across positions. The top and middle highlighted windows are positioned farther to the right than the lower ones, indicating a sliding progression. The text “Mistral 7B” appears in bold at the lower right.

Sliding Window Attention

[FIGURE] A diagram with three horizontal rows of small vertical rectangles. In each row, most rectangles are pale yellow/orange and arranged evenly across the row. At the far right of each row there is a salmon-colored rectangle labeled “query” beneath it. In the top row, a black rounded rectangle highlights only the two rightmost pale yellow/orange rectangles immediately to the left of the query. In the middle row, a long black rounded rectangle spans nearly the full row, enclosing all pale yellow/orange rectangles from left to right, with the query outside the highlight on the far right. In the bottom row, a black rounded rectangle again highlights only the two rightmost pale yellow/orange rectangles immediately to the left of the query. The bottom-right corner contains the text “GPT-OSS”.

Streaming LLM

https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.17453

[FIGURE] Top schematic: a horizontal sequence of token-like rectangles. On the left, one orange rectangle is enclosed in a black rounded rectangle. Several orange rectangles follow to the right. Farther right, a larger black rounded rectangle encloses three orange rectangles. At the far right is a single pink/orange rectangle labeled “query”. The diagram visually suggests a streaming context with a selected block/window and a later query token.

[FIGURE] Bottom left legend with three colored line labels:
- Blue line: “Dense Attention”
- Orange line: “Window Attention”
- Red line: “StreamingLLM”

[FIGURE] Bottom center-left line chart titled “Llama-2-7B”. Y-axis label: “logPPL”. X-axis label: “Input Length”, with ticks labeled approximately 0K, 5K, 10K, 15K, 20K. Three curves are shown: blue (Dense Attention), orange (Window Attention), red (StreamingLLM). Two vertical dashed reference lines appear near the left side, labeled vertically “KV Cache Size” and “Pre-training Length”. The blue curve stays near ~8–9 but has noticeable spikes around mid input lengths, reaching above 10 and near 12. The orange curve rises from about 1.7 to around 8–9 near the left, then remains roughly around 8.5–9 with small fluctuations. The red curve stays low around ~1.5–2 across the full range with minor variation.

[FIGURE] Bottom right line chart titled “Pythia-12B”. Y-axis label: “logPPL”. X-axis label: “Input Length”, with ticks labeled approximately 0K, 5K, 10K, 15K, 20K. Three curves are shown: blue (Dense Attention), orange (Window Attention), red (StreamingLLM). Two vertical dashed reference lines appear near the left side, labeled vertically “KV Cache Size” and “Pre-training Length”. The blue curve starts around ~2, rises sharply after the early region, and then stays around ~6–7.5 with fluctuations. The orange curve rises to around ~3–4 near the left, then fluctuates around ~2.5–3 for most of the range. The red curve stays around ~2 with small fluctuations throughout.

⼤量 attention

[FIGURE]
Three horizontal attention-map plots are shown on the right side of the slide, stacked vertically. Each plot has the y-axis labeled “Attention Head” and the x-axis labeled “Sequence Position,” with many vertical tick labels along the sequence axis. The plots use a light green background with darker green vertical marks/spikes indicating attention concentrated at certain sequence positions across multiple heads.

The top plot is labeled “(a) Attention map at position 178.” It shows a few prominent darker green vertical bands/spikes at selected positions, with a denser cluster of darker marks toward the right side of the sequence.

The middle plot is labeled “(b) Attention map at position 228.” It shows more scattered darker green vertical marks across the sequence, again with several stronger bands and a cluster of marks near the right end.

The bottom plot is labeled “(c) Attention map at position 278.” It shows the densest pattern of darker green vertical marks, with many spikes across the sequence and a heavy concentration toward the far right end.

Scissorhands: https://
arxiv.org/abs/2305.17118

[FIGURE] A 2×2 grid of line charts showing performance versus Compression for language modeling and five-shot evaluation.

[FIGURE] (a) Language Modeling. Line chart with y-axis labeled “Perplexity” ranging roughly from 7.5 to 10.0 and x-axis labeled “Compression” with tick labels 1X, 2X, 3X, 4X, 5X. Three lines are shown with legend: OPT-13B (blue), OPT-30B (orange), OPT-66B (green). Perplexity increases slightly as compression increases for all three models; OPT-13B is highest, OPT-30B is in the middle, and OPT-66B is lowest across the plotted range.

[FIGURE] (b) OPT-6B Five shot. Line chart with y-axis labeled “Accuracy” from 0.0 to 1.0 and x-axis labeled “Compression” with tick labels 1X, 2X, 3X, 4X, 5X. Four lines are shown with legend: Hellaswag (orange), MathQA (green), PIQA (red), Winogrande (purple). PIQA is highest around 0.78 and nearly flat; Hellaswag is around 0.70 and declines slightly at higher compression; Winogrande is around 0.60 and nearly flat; MathQA is around 0.20 and nearly flat.

[FIGURE] (c) OPT-13B Five shot. Line chart with y-axis labeled “Accuracy” from 0.0 to 1.0 and x-axis labeled “Compression” with tick labels 1X, 2X, 3X, 4X, 5X. Four lines are shown with legend: Hellaswag (orange), MathQA (green), PIQA (red), Winogrande (purple). PIQA is around 0.78 and nearly flat with a slight decrease at 5X; Hellaswag is around 0.72 at low compression and trends down to about 0.69 by 3X; Winogrande is around 0.61 and gradually decreases to about 0.59; MathQA is around 0.20 and flat.

[FIGURE] (d) OPT-30B Five shot. Line chart with y-axis labeled “Accuracy” from 0.0 to 1.0 and x-axis labeled “Compression” with tick labels 1X, 2X, 3X, 4X, 5X. Four lines are shown with legend: Hellaswag (orange), MathQA (green), PIQA (red), Winogrande (purple). PIQA is around 0.78 and nearly flat; Hellaswag is around 0.75–0.76 at lower compression and declines to about 0.70 by 5X; Winogrande is around 0.65 and slowly decreases to about 0.63; MathQA is around 0.20 and flat.

[FIGURE] A diagram with two horizontal rows of seven vertical token blocks each.  
Top row tokens, left to right: 「大」「家」「好」「我」「是」「大」「金」, each shown as a stacked rectangle with a light blue upper half and orange lower half.  
Between the top and bottom rows, black downward arrows appear under the first five tokens and under the last token, indicating copying from the top sequence to the bottom sequence. The token 「金」 on the top row has a red X/cross mark over the arrow below it, indicating it is not copied in the same way as the others.  
Bottom row tokens, left to right: 「大」「家」「好」「我」「是」「小」「金」, each shown as stacked rectangles; the last two bottom rectangles have a different lower-half color (pink/red-orange) compared with the earlier orange lower halves, while their upper halves remain light blue. The layout visually contrasts the top sequence ending in 「大金」 with the bottom sequence ending in 「小金」.

A pricing table for “Text tokens” showing prices per 1M tokens, with a tab row labeled Standard, Batch, Flex, Priority (Standard appears selected). Under “Our latest models,” the table lists columns for Model, then Short context with Input, Cached input, Output, and Long context with Input, Cached input, Output. [FIGURE]
A dark “Original prompt” panel containing a grid of white circles, triangles, and squares arranged in rows and columns. From this panel, arrows lead to two panels on the right: the top panel labeled “Cache hit” with a green check icon and mostly green shapes in the same grid pattern, plus two gray shapes at the far right of the bottom row; the bottom panel labeled “Cache miss” with an orange X icon and mostly gray shapes in the same grid pattern, plus one orange shape at the top-left and the rest gray. The arrows visually indicate the original prompt branching to either a cache hit or cache miss outcome.

甚麼時候跨對話的 Cache 可以發揮作⽤

[FIGURE] 左側是一支手機圖示，旁邊有綠色文字框「我是小金 ...」與藍色文字框「自我介紹」，兩者之間以左右雙向箭頭表示資料往返；下方文字寫「使用 AI Agent 的 時候!」。整體表示在使用 AI Agent 時，跨對話內容可能被反覆帶入。

[FIGURE] 中間是一個螃蟹圖示，代表中介/代理角色。手機與螃蟹之間有雙向箭頭，螃蟹下方有一個向上的黑色箭頭，箭頭下方文字為「自身相關資訊 的文字檔」，表示有一份關於自身資訊的文字檔輸入到這個流程中。

[FIGURE] 右側是一個綠框的大方塊，內寫「語言 模型」，代表語言模型。螃蟹與語言模型之間有雙向箭頭，右側上方有綠色文字框「我是小金 ...」，下方有黃色文字框寫「我是小金 ... / 人生目 / 標 ....」，再下方有藍色文字框「自我介紹」。黃色框右側有一條虛線箭頭指向文字「System prompt」，表示該內容被作為 system prompt。右下角文字寫「每次呼叫語言模型 的 prompt 都會附上」，其下以紅字強調「Cache Hit!」。整體表示每次呼叫語言模型時都會附帶相同的 prompt/system prompt，因此可觸發跨對話快取命中。

甚麼時候跨對話的 Cache 可以發揮作⽤
